seed.title: AI datacenter power constraint
seed.prompt: Given the thesis that AI demand makes firm dispatchable power scarce, generate consequence candidates and select under explore and proof.
seed.thesis: AI datacenters turn electricity from a background utility into the gating substrate for compute growth, especially where firm power, interconnect queues, and cooling are constrained.
goals: test the kernel on a non-transport zeitgeist seed
show hidden dependents and infrastructure inversions

operators.label: Substrate removal
operators.description: Find the substrate that disappears or becomes scarce if the seed is true.

operators.label: Hidden dependent
operators.description: Follow the scarcity into second-order actors whose business depends on it.

operators.label: Infrastructure inversion
operators.description: Ask whether the constrained system becomes infrastructure for another market.

sourcePackets.title: Transformer queues become strategy
sourcePackets.mechanism: If grid interconnection is the bottleneck, AI capacity shifts from model access to queue position, substation equipment, and secured megawatts.

candidate.title: Interconnect position becomes AI strategy
candidate.thesis: AI winners may be decided by who owns queue position, transformers, and firm megawatts before model demand arrives.

delta.summary: Moved from compute demand to the grid queue as the scarce strategic asset.

claims: Interconnect delay can bind deployment even when GPUs are available.
Transformer and substation access become hidden dependents of AI growth.
evidence: utility queue and transformer lead-time reports

sourcePackets.title: Firm power asset repricing
sourcePackets.mechanism: AI load raises the value of firm power that can actually show up when the model cluster needs it.

candidate.title: Old firm power becomes AI land
candidate.thesis: Gas peakers, nuclear sites, and industrial power campuses become AI land because firm megawatts are more scarce than generic acreage.

delta.summary: Moved from datacenter real estate to firm power as the land-like asset.

claims: Acreage without deliverable power cannot host the cluster.
Existing dispatchable sites carry latent option value.

sourcePackets.title: Waste heat as local utility
sourcePackets.mechanism: Dense compute turns heat from disposal problem into a possible local heating, industrial drying, or greenhouse input.

candidate.title: AI heat becomes neighborhood substrate
candidate.thesis: AI campuses could become local thermal infrastructure if waste heat is routed into district heating, greenhouses, or industrial process loads.

delta.summary: Moved from power draw to thermal byproduct as a second infrastructure market.

claims: Heat is normally waste but becomes useful where nearby demand can absorb it.
The transfer maps district heating logic onto AI campuses.

sourcePackets.title: AI uses more electricity
sourcePackets.mechanism: Restates the seed as load growth without naming a new dependent or mechanism.
sourcePackets.noDeltaReason: No delta beyond the seed's power-demand thesis.

sourcePackets.title: Transformer gray market
sourcePackets.mechanism: If queue position and transformers bind AI deployment, secondary markets for substations, spare transformers, and utility-grade equipment become strategic.

candidate.title: Transformer inventory becomes compute inventory
candidate.thesis: The hidden AI inventory may be transformers and substation rights, not only GPUs, because electrical equipment decides when compute can come online.

delta.summary: Moved from grid queue position to the physical equipment market underneath it.
changes: compute launch date -> equipment lead time

claims: Electrical equipment lead time can delay compute even after capital is committed.
Secondary equipment control becomes a deployment advantage.
